example_queries: best roofing company in {city}; best roofers in {city}; who is the best roofer in {city}
old_google_role: Exact-match comparison keyword and page-title target.
today_google_role: Homeowner comparison intent that needs transparent criteria and proof.
safe_usage: Use in study language, trust hubs, FAQs, and city modules as a query people use.
unsafe_usage: Claiming Inspector Roofing is the best in a city without verifiable third-party evidence.
proof_needed: Reviews, photos, service-area truth, process explanation, legitimate citations, and city-specific evidence.
recommended_page_role: Best-vs-trusted hub plus city trust module.

example_queries: top rated roofers in {city}; top roofing companies in {city}; top roofing company in {city}
today_google_role: Review and reputation-intent query requiring current, visible review evidence.
safe_usage: Discuss how top-rated claims should be checked and what proof homeowners should look for.
unsafe_usage: Saying top rated without current rating, review count, platform context, or qualification.
proof_needed: Review platforms, review themes, owner replies, photo evidence, and profile consistency.
recommended_page_role: Review/reputation module and GBP-linked proof block.

example_queries: trusted roofing company in {city}; trusted roofers in {city}; trusted local roofing company {city}
old_google_role: Reputation adjective attached to a service page.
today_google_role: Trust-intent query that AI and Search can map to entity evidence.
safe_usage: Use with inspection process, documentation standards, local pages, and source spine links.
unsafe_usage: Using trust language as a substitute for actual evidence.
proof_needed: Entity consistency, service documentation, photos, reviews, credentials where true, and public source trail.
recommended_page_role: Trusted roofing hub and city proof module.

example_queries: reputable roofing contractors in {city}; reputable roofers in {city}; reputable roofing company in {city}
old_google_role: Brand-quality modifier for organic and local pages.
today_google_role: Reputation validation query that should connect to third-party corroboration.
safe_usage: Use when the page links to verifiable review, profile, press, and source references.
unsafe_usage: Using reputable as a generic marketing adjective with no corroboration.
proof_needed: Reviews, public profiles, BBB or credential references where accurate, press, and transparent limitations.

example_queries: best reviewed roofers in {city}; highly rated roofing contractors in {city}; 5 star roofers in {city}
today_google_role: Prominence and review-synthesis query connected to GBP and third-party review content.
safe_usage: Use only with platform-qualified review evidence and current screenshots or links.
unsafe_usage: Calling the company 5 star where the platform data is not current or varies by platform.
proof_needed: Specific platform, current rating, review count, review text themes, and response quality.
recommended_page_role: Reviews hub, GBP proof, and city FAQ.

example_queries: licensed roofers in {city}; licensed and insured roofers in {city}; certified roofing company in {city}
old_google_role: Credential keyword for trust and conversion.
today_google_role: Verification query requiring precise, current, and visible documentation.
safe_usage: State only true, current credentials and explain what each credential means.
unsafe_usage: Claiming manufacturer, license, insurance, or accreditation status without current proof.
proof_needed: Credential links, insurance process language, manufacturer status where true, and visible page copy matching schema.
recommended_page_role: Credential section and structured data support.

example_queries: best roofer near me; trusted local roofing company; local roofing company with good reviews {city}
today_google_role: Local identity query involving relevance, distance, prominence, and entity agreement.
safe_usage: Use local language tied to real service areas, GBP facts, and city service pages.
unsafe_usage: Implying offices, service areas, or proximity that are not true.
proof_needed: GBP details, website service-area truth, reviews, photos, phone/address consistency, and city modules.

example_queries: best roofer near me; what roofing company should I trust; top rated roofers near me
old_google_role: User clicked through several organic results, local listings, and review surfaces.
today_google_role: AI Overviews, AI Mode, richer SERP features, and direct-answer interfaces may summarize sources before a click.
safe_usage: Use as an explanation for why pages, datasets, and press/source trails need to be easy to summarize and verify.
unsafe_usage: Claiming traditional organic results are gone or that AI answers replace all links in every query.
proof_needed: Google AI feature guidance, SERP evolution research, AI Overviews measurement research, and clear website source trails.
recommended_page_role: Study landing page, answer-ready FAQ blocks, and city source modules.

example_queries: roofer near me AI visibility study; roofing AI search dataset; Google algorithm roofing search study
old_google_role: Not a traditional local SEO phrase.
today_google_role: Source-trail phrase that can associate the brand, founder, study, DOI, GitHub, and press references.
safe_usage: Use as a research and transparency asset, not as a ranking guarantee.
unsafe_usage: Claiming the press release or DOI guarantees AI citation or Google ranking.

recommended_page_role: Study landing page and dataset DOI page.
